heading: Depository institutions lacking Federal deposit insurance
label: 1831t Depository institutions lacking Federal deposit insurance
notes: References in TextThe Consumer Financial Protection Act of 2010, referred to in subsec. (f)(1), is title X of , , , which enacted subchapter V (§ 5481 et seq.) of chapter 53 of this title and enacted, amended, and repealed numerous other sections and notes in the Code. Subtitle B of the Act is classified generally to part B (§ 5511 et seq.) of subchapter V of chapter 53 of this title. For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see Short Title note set out under and Tables.Pub. (f)(1), is , , which is classified generally to subchapter I (§ 41 et seq.) of chapter 2 of Title 15, Commerce and Trade. For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see and Tables.act Sept. 26, 1914, ch. 31138 Stat. 717section 58 of Title 15

Amendments2015—Subsec. (a)(2)(A)(iii). added cl. (iii).Pub. L. 114–942010—Subsecs. (c), (d), (e)(2)(B). –(C)(i), substituted “Bureau” for “Federal Trade Commission”.Pub. L. 111–203, § 1090(2)(A)Subsec. (e)(5). , added par. (5).Pub. L. 111–203, § 1090(2)(C)(ii)Subsec. (f)(1). , added par. (1) and struck out former par. (1). Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “Compliance with the requirements of subsections (b), (c) and (e), and any regulation prescribed or order issued under any such subsection, shall be enforced under the Federal Trade Commission Act by the Federal Trade Commission.”Pub. L. 111–203, § 1090(2)(D)(i)Subsec. (f)(2)(C). , added subpar. (C) and struck out former subpar. (C). Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “If the Federal Trade Commission has instituted an enforcement action for a violation of this section, no appropriate State supervisor may, during the pendency of such action, bring an action under this section against any defendant named in the complaint of the Commission for any violation of this section that is alleged in that complaint.”Pub. L. 111–203, § 1090(2)(D)(ii)2006—Subsec. (a)(3). , added par. (3).Pub. L. 109–351, § 505(a)Subsec. (b)(1). , substituted “or share certificate.” for “or similar instrument evidencing a deposit”.Pub. L. 109–351, § 505(b)Subsec. (b)(2). , amended heading and text generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “Include conspicuously in all advertising and at each place where deposits are normally received a notice that the institution is not federally insured.”Pub. L. 109–351, § 505(c)Subsec. (b)(3). , amended par. (3) generally. Prior to amendment, par. (3) related to acknowledgement of disclosure and consisted of subpars. (A) to (C).Pub. L. 109–351, § 505(d)Subsec. (c). , amended heading and text generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “To ensure that current and prospective customers understand the risks involved in foregoing Federal deposit insurance, the Federal Trade Commission, by regulation or order, shall prescribe the manner and content of disclosure required under this section.”Pub. L. 109–351, § 505(e)Subsec. (d). substituted “an amount equal to the standard maximum deposit insurance amount” for “$100,000”.Pub. L. 109–173Subsec. (e). , redesignated subsec. (f) as (e) and struck out former subsec. (e) which related to eligibility for Federal deposit insurance.Pub. L. 109–351, § 505(f)Subsec. (f). , amended heading and text generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “Compliance with the requirements of this section, and any regulation prescribed or order issued under this section, shall be enforced under the Federal Trade Commission Act by the Federal Trade Commission.”Pub. L. 109–351, § 505(g), redesignated subsec. (g) as (f). Former subsec. (f) redesignated (e).Pub. L. 109–351, § 505(f)(2)Subsec. (g). , redesignated subsec. (g) as (f).Pub. L. 109–351, § 505(f)(2)1994—Subsec. (b)(3). amended heading and text of subsec. (b)(3) generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “Receive deposits only for the account of persons who have signed a written acknowledgment that the institution is not federally insured, and that if the institution fails, the Federal Government does not guarantee that they will get back their money.”Pub. L. 103–325

Effective Date of 2006 AmendmentAmendment by effective , see , set out as a note under .Pub. L. 109–173section 2(e) of Pub. L. 109–173section 1785 of this titleApr. 1, 2006

Effective Date of 1994 Amendment, , , provided that:Pub. L. 103–325, title III, § 340(b)108 Stat. 2238Sept. 23, 1994“Section 43(b)(3) of the Federal Deposit Insurance Act [], as amended by subsection (a), shall take effect in accordance with section 151(a)(2)(D) of the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation Improvement Act of 1991 [see Effective Date note below].”12 U.S.C. 1831t(b)(3)

Effective Date, , , provided that:Pub. L. 102–242, title I, § 151(a)(2)105 Stat. 2284Dec. 19, 1991“Section 40 of the Federal Deposit Insurance Act [] (as added by paragraph (1)) shall become effective on the date of enactment of this Act [], except that—12 U.S.C. 1831tDec. 19, 1991“(A)paragraphs (1) and (2) of subsection (b) shall become effective 1 year after the date of enactment of this Act;“(B)during the period beginning 1 year after that date of enactment of this Act and ending 30 months after that date of enactment, subsection (b)(1) shall apply with ‘, and that if the institution fails, the Federal Government does not guarantee that depositors will get back their money’ omitted;“(C)subsection (e) shall become effective 2 years after that date of enactment; and“(D)subsection (b)(3) shall become effective 30 months after that date of enactment.”

Viability of Private Deposit Insurers, , , as amended by , , , provided that:Pub. L. 102–242, title I, § 151(b)105 Stat. 2285Pub. L. 102–550, title XVI, § 1603(f)(1)106 Stat. 4081Dec. 19, 1991Oct. 28, 1992“(1).—Deadline for initial independent auditThe initial annual audit under section 43(a)(1) of the Federal Deposit Insurance Act [] (as added by subsection (a)) shall be completed not later than 120 days after the date of enactment of this Act [].12 U.S.C. 1831t(a)(1)Dec. 19, 1991“(2).—Business plan requiredNot later than 240 days after the date of enactment of this Act [], any private deposit insurer shall provide a business plan to each appropriate supervisor of each State in which deposits are received by any depository institution lacking Federal deposit insurance the deposits of which are insured by a private deposit insurer. The business plan shall explain in detail why the private deposit insurer is viable, and shall, at a minimum—Dec. 19, 1991“(A)describe the insurer’s—“(i)underwriting standards;“(ii)resources, including trends in and forecasts of assets, income, and expenses;“(iii)risk-management program, including examination and supervision, problem case resolution, and remedies; and“(B)include, for the preceding 5 years, copies of annual audits, annual reports, and annual meeting agendas and minutes.“(3).—DefinitionsFor purposes of this subsection, the terms ‘appropriate supervisor’, ‘depository institution’, ‘lacking Federal deposit insurance’, and ‘private deposit insurer’ have the same meaning as in section 43(f) of the Federal Deposit Insurance Act [] (as added by subsection (a)).”12 U.S.C. 1831t(f)

children.heading: Annual independent audit of private deposit insurers
children.label: a Annual independent audit of private deposit insurers

children.heading: Audit required
children.label: 1 Audit required
children.text: Any private deposit insurer shall obtain an annual audit from an independent auditor using generally accepted auditing standards. The audit shall include a determination of whether the private deposit insurer follows generally accepted accounting principles and has set aside sufficient reserves for losses.

children.label: i
children.text: to each depository institution the deposits of which are insured by the private deposit insurer, not later than 14 days after the audit is completed;

children.label: ii
children.text: to the appropriate supervisory agency of each State in which such an institution receives deposits, not later than 7 days after the audit is completed; and

children.label: iii
children.text: in the case of depository institutions described in subsection (e)(2)(A) the deposits of which are insured by the private insurer which are members of a Federal home loan bank, to the Federal Housing Finance Agency, not later than 7 days after the audit is completed.

children.heading: Depository institution
children.label: B Depository institution
children.text: Any depository institution the deposits of which are insured by the private deposit insurer shall provide a copy of the audit report, upon request, to any current or prospective customer of the institution.

children.heading: Enforcement by appropriate State supervisor
children.label: 3 Enforcement by appropriate State supervisor
children.text: Any appropriate State supervisor of a private deposit insurer, and any appropriate State supervisor of a depository institution which receives deposits that are insured by a private deposit insurer, may examine and enforce compliance with this subsection under the applicable regulatory authority of such supervisor.

children.heading: Periodic statements; account records
children.label: 1 Periodic statements; account records
children.text: Include conspicuously in all periodic statements of account, on each signature card, and on each passbook, certificate of deposit, or share certificate. a notice that the institution is not federally insured, and that if the institution fails, the Federal Government does not guarantee that depositors will get back their money.1So in original. The period probably should not appear.1

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Include clearly and conspicuously in all advertising, except as provided in subparagraph (B); and at each station or window where deposits are normally received, its principal place of business and all its branches where it accepts deposits or opens accounts (excluding automated teller machines or point of sale terminals), and on its main Internet page, a notice that the institution is not federally insured.

children.label: i
children.text: Any sign, document, or other item that contains the name of the depository institution, its logo, or its contact information, but only if the sign, document, or item does not include any information about the institution’s products or services or information otherwise promoting the institution.

children.label: ii
children.text: Small utilitarian items that do not mention deposit products or insurance if inclusion of the notice would be impractical.

children.heading: New depositors obtained other than through a conversion or merger
children.label: A New depositors obtained other than through a conversion or merger
i
children.text: the institution is not federally insured; and

children.label: ii
children.text: if the institution fails, the Federal Government does not guarantee that the depositor will get back the depositor’s money.

children.heading: New depositors obtained through a conversion or merger
children.label: B New depositors obtained through a conversion or merger
i
children.text: the depositor has signed a written acknowledgement described in subparagraph (A); or

children.label: ii
children.text: the institution makes an attempt, as described in subparagraph (D) and sent by mail no later than 45 days after the effective date of the conversion or merger, to obtain the acknowledgment.

children.label: i
children.text: the depositor has signed a written acknowledgement described in subparagraph (A); or

children.label: ii
children.text: the institution has complied with the provisions of subparagraph (E) which are applicable as of the date of the deposit.

children.heading: Alternative provision of notice to new depositors obtained through a conversion or merger
children.label: D Alternative provision of notice to new depositors obtained through a conversion or merger

children.heading: In general2So in original. No cl. (ii) has been enacted.2
children.label: i In general2So in original. No cl. (ii) has been enacted.2
I
children.text: a conspicuous card containing the information described in clauses (i) and (ii) of subparagraph (A), and a line for the signature of the depositor; and

children.label: II
children.text: accompanying materials requesting the depositor to sign the card, and return the signed card to the institution.

children.heading: Alternative provision of notice to current depositors
children.label: E Alternative provision of notice to current depositors
I
children.text: a conspicuous card containing the information described in clauses (i) and (ii) of subparagraph (A), and a line for the signature of the depositor; and

children.label: II
children.text: accompanying materials requesting the depositor to sign the card, and return the signed card to the institution.

children.heading: Manner and timing of notice
children.label: ii Manner and timing of notice

children.heading: First notice
children.label: I First notice
children.text: Make the transmission described in clause (i) via mail not later than three months after .October 13, 2006

children.heading: Second notice
children.label: II Second notice
children.text: Make a second transmission described in clause (i) via mail not less than 30 days and not more than three months after a transmission to the depositor in accordance with subclause (I), if the institution has not, by the date of such mailing, received from the depositor a card referred to in clause (i) which has been signed by the depositor.

children.heading: Manner and content of disclosure
children.label: c Manner and content of disclosure
children.text: To ensure that current and prospective customers understand the risks involved in foregoing Federal deposit insurance, the Bureau, by regulation or order, shall prescribe the manner and content of disclosure required under this section, which shall be presented in such format and in such type size and manner as to be simple and easy to understand.

children.heading: Exceptions for institutions not receiving retail deposits
children.label: d Exceptions for institutions not receiving retail deposits
children.text: The Bureau may, by regulation or order, make exceptions to subsection (b) for any depository institution that, within the United States, does not receive initial deposits of less than an amount equal to the standard maximum deposit insurance amount from individuals who are citizens or residents of the United States, other than money received in connection with any draft or similar instrument issued to transmit money.

children.heading: Appropriate supervisor
children.label: 1 Appropriate supervisor
children.text: The “appropriate supervisor” of a depository institution means the agency primarily responsible for supervising the institution.

children.label: A
children.text: any entity described in ; andsection 461(b)(1)(A)(iv) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: is engaged in the business of receiving deposits; and

children.label: ii
children.text: could reasonably be mistaken for a depository institution by the entity’s current or prospective customers.

children.label: A
children.text: an insured depository institution; or

children.label: B
children.text: an insured credit union, as defined in section 101 of the Federal Credit Union Act [].12 U.S.C. 1752

children.heading: Private deposit insurer
children.label: 4 Private deposit insurer
children.text: The term “private deposit insurer” means any entity insuring the deposits of any depository institution lacking Federal deposit insurance.

children.heading: Bureau
children.label: 5 Bureau
children.text: The term “Bureau” means the Bureau of Consumer Financial Protection.

children.heading: Limited enforcement authority
children.label: 1 Limited enforcement authority
children.text: Compliance with the requirements of subsections (b), (c), and (e), and any regulation prescribed or order issued under such subsection, shall be enforced under the Consumer Financial Protection Act of 2010, by the Bureau, subject to subtitle B of the Consumer Financial Protection Act of 2010 [ et seq.], and under the Federal Trade Commission Act ( et seq.) by the Federal Trade Commission.12 U.S.C. 551115 U.S.C. 41

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (C), an appropriate State supervisor of a depository institution lacking Federal deposit insurance may examine and enforce compliance with the requirements of this section, and any regulation prescribed under this section.

children.heading: State powers
children.label: B State powers
children.text: For purposes of bringing any action to enforce compliance with this section, no provision of this section shall be construed as preventing an appropriate State supervisor of a depository institution lacking Federal deposit insurance from exercising any powers conferred on such official by the laws of such State.

children.heading: Limitation on State action while Federal action pending
children.label: C Limitation on State action while Federal action pending
children.text: If the Bureau or Federal Trade Commission has instituted an enforcement action for a violation of this section, no appropriate State supervisory agency may, during the pendency of such action, bring an action under this section against any defendant named in the complaint of the Bureau or Federal Trade Commission for any violation of this section that is alleged in that complaint.
